title: fft->ifft data-flow chain corrupts ONLY inside a --shared-lib (precompiled OptNone) function; identical source in user code works
goal: Found by sweep A (PR #130). A function compiled into a precompiled --shared-lib (e.g. stdlib fast-convolve) that chains fft->ifft corrupts results (#(5.1e9 -8 -8 -8)); the SAME source as ordinary user code (even with (require stdlib)) is correct. Isolated: fft alone, complex-multiply, double/int division, and ifft's conjugate/scale halves each work precompiled in isolation; only the full fft->ifft data-flow chain inside an OptNone/--shared-lib function fails. Minimal repro (signal_fft.md): (define (chain a) (ifft (fft a))) built via eshkol-run --shared-lib -o onlyfft onlyfft.esk => (chain #(1. 2. 3. 4.)) garbage. Likely an OptNone / precompiled-module codegen difference (buffer reuse or arena/temp aliasing that -O and normal user-compile don't hit).
acceptance: fast-convolve and the chain repro correct in --shared-lib builds; -r AND AOT; no regression to normal fft/ifft
